description_long: This crustacean features an intricate arrangement of elongated appendages and filaments, primarily in deep blue and coppery orange hues. Its body is compact and shield-like, with a central brown carapace that is flanked by numerous slender, curved limbs radiating outward. The appendages are both rigid and flexible, some arching gracefully while others splay out in a more chaotic fashion, giving the creature a dynamic, almost feathery appearance. The head region is adorned with delicate, thread-like antennae and possibly small, subtle eyes, though they are not distinctly visible. The overall form is symmetrical, with the appendages creating a sense of motion and complexity. This crustacean would stand out in an urban environment due to its vivid coloration and ornate structure.
description: A striking crustacean with a brown central carapace and radiating blue and orange appendages, creating a feathery, ornate appearance. Its limbs and antennae give it a dynamic, intricate look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the symmetrical arrangement of blue and orange appendages fanning out from a central brown carapace, with antennae and fine limbs visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the length and curvature of the appendages, showing how they arch and extend backward from the body.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the broad, shield-like carapace and the splayed arrangement of limbs, emphasizing the symmetry and layered structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, displaying the flowing, elongated appendages and the layered, feathery effect.
